Hi there,

If you want to know more about the extradition bill controversy in HK, scroll down for info.

***Update Timeline of the whole anti-extradition movement
https://hkrev.info/

About the Bill
Government info
https://www.legco.gov.hk/yr18-19/english/panels/se/agenda/se20190603.htm

Local backlash

Hong Kong Bar Association has voiced out against this piece of legislation for multiple times

Brief guide
https://bit.ly/2Ip6QLD

Observations
https://bit.ly/2IFOPdU
https://bit.ly/2WD2gU7

Milder response from the Law Society of Hong Kong
http://www.hklawsoc.org.hk/pub_c/news/submissions/20190605.pdf

Note that Hong Kong has been treating PRC and Taiwan as two separate jurisdiction for quite a number of laws.

Examples include:
Cap. 10 PROBATE AND ADMINISTRATION ORDINANCE
// 17(3) In this section, “Mainland” (內地) means any part of China other than Hong Kong, Macau and Taiwan.//

Cap. 434 MERCHANT SHIPPING (LIMITATION OF SHIPOWNERS LIABILITY) ORDINANCE
// 2. “the Mainland” (內地) means any part of China other than Hong Kong, Macau and Taiwan. //

Cap. 597 MAINLAND JUDGMENTS (RECIPROCAL ENFORCEMENT) ORDINANCE
// 2. “Mainland” (內地) means any part of China other than Hong Kong, Macau and Taiwan //

Cap. 609 Arbitration Ordinance
// 2(1). the Mainland (內地) means any part of China other than Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan. //


International criticisms
Numerous legal associations have jointly issued an opinion piece, criticising the extradition bill as ”ill-conceived proposals” that “fundamentally imperil the operation of the rule of law in
Hong Kong.”, stressing that the ‘political offence exception’ inside the extradition bill proposal is // widely recognised to be hollow and redundant. Persecuting states do not realistically advertise the ‘political’ nature of their allegations. It is a ‘protection’ not found in most contemporary extradition treaties. //

https://www.fairtrials.org/sites/default/files/publication_pdf/Joint-observations-human-rights-implications-Hong-Kong-Fugitive-Offenders-Mutual-Legal-Assistance-Criminal-Matters-Legislation.pdf

We Hongkongers will not yield until the regime satisfies our 5 demands:
— Fully withdraw (instead of “postpone”) the extradition bill
— Retract the official characterisation of the protest as “riot”
— Release all arrested protesters and promise that they won’t be prosecuted in the future

— Universal suffrage for both chief executive and legislative council elections
